lines.text: Untouched. The dungeon has not yet decided what you are worth.
Your footsteps are too loud. Confidence echoes.
Unbloodied this deep. The dungeon finds this interesting. So does something below.
The ones who reach the lower floors without wounds either know something others don't — or haven't been properly tested yet.
The dungeon has noted your intact condition. It is revising its approach accordingly.
Full of life and descending into what took it from so many others. That particular courage, or foolishness, has always been interesting to watch.
Marked, but standing. The dungeon respects wounds that didn't stop the descent.
You have been tested. You have not been broken. The distinction matters here.
Every scar is a question answered in flesh. The dungeon charges interest in blood — you have been paying.
Damaged enough to be dangerous. Not damaged enough to be desperate. The most useful state for what lies ahead.
The ones who keep moving at this condition are the ones the dungeon takes seriously. You have earned its full attention.
Blood on the pages. Every answer costs more than the last.
Your hands shake. The next question will be harder to read.
The dungeon feeds on what you lose. It is well-fed.
This is the stage where most seekers make their final mistake. The descent narrows. The errors compound.
Barely. That is the word for what you are, right now, in this place. Barely enough. The dungeon is watching to see if barely holds.
Not yet finished. The dungeon seems almost disappointed by that.
The margin between you and the archive below has never been thinner. Answer carefully.
